- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

logs.name: greedy-graphite
logs.text:
Ah yes the totally legitimate dossier saying he hires whores to piss on Obamas bed and watches Japanese animated pornogrphy in footie pajamas. 

Mhm that totally a real document not just the bitter back biting of someone hired by the Clinton's to falsify. 

Schiff the man whose fundraisers are run by Ukrainian arms dealers I wonder if the made up nation of Ukraine is pulling his shirt hairs for gables to invent these things. Schiff should be arrested and hanged a traitor for working with the Ukrainian arms dealers. 

The bitter clingers in the Congress must be removed and indefinitely detained for the good of the nation. McCain is a senile coward and has always been. 


The Democrats are mad that nunes though showed that the fbi was spying on trump and his team to invent these issues and the Democrats commit the worse offense since Watergate. 

Makes me question why we continue to tolerate Democrats or the Democratic party instead of rounding them up for crimes against the fatherland

logs.name: straight-infrared
logs.text:
Okay lets start talking about a few of the things on your list of things that need to be addressed.

The Steele Dossier: Okay if the golden showers thing is all you took away from that dossier you literally focused on the LEAST important part taking up a few paragraphs in a 35 page doccument, and the  author states in the document was the least verified part that he had. The really important part was the the Rosneft deal. In which the dossier states that Trump was offered a 19% deal in Rosneft for running. In december [19.5%](http://washingtonmonthly.com/2017/01/11/mnuchin-needs-to-explain-the-19-5-sale-of-rosneft/) of the company was privatised, with unknown owners. On top of that there have been MANY other points within the document that have been verified.  Since it came out reporters have been able to verify a LARGE portion of the document's [listed communications](http://www.cnn.com/2017/02/10/politics/russia-dossier-update/), and its kinda suspicious that a few sources connected with the document have been [murdered](http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/2017/01/27/mystery-death-ex-kgb-chief-linked-mi6-spys-dossier-donald-trump/) (a bit of a putin hallmark). 

Next is the Pinging of the Russian Bank. Alfa Bank has long been implicated in Russian Money laundering schemes, but to note its not that the domain was registered with the trump organization, its that the physical server was regestered with at the headquarters of the Trump Organization. You may want to [reread the actual original implications](http://www.slate.com/articles/news_and_politics/cover_story/2016/10/was_a_server_registered_to_the_trump_organization_communicating_with_russia.html) by the computer scientists who found it. And it should be noted that Trump's real estate businesses have been tied to a recently descovered [Russian Money Laundering Scheme](http://www.newsweek.com/how-russians-launder-stolen-money-real-estate-407810). A scheme in which the whistle blower is already [dead](https://www.nytimes.com/2017/03/21/world/europe/russia-sergei-magnitsky-william-browder.html?_r=0) So yeah it may not be communication with the Trump over the elections but there are OTHER criminal possibilities and buisness ties on the table.

Next we have the problem of Trump's staff and advisors heavy connections with Russia. [Flynn](https://www.nytimes.com/2017/02/13/us/politics/donald-trump-national-security-adviser-michael-flynn.html); [Manafort](https://www.apnews.com/122ae0b5848345faa88108a03de40c5a), [Sessions](http://www.npr.org/sections/thetwo-way/2017/03/01/518058002/ag-sessions-talks-with-russian-envoy-may-be-conflict-with-senate-testimony); [Tillerson](https://www.theatlantic.com/international/archive/2017/01/tillerson-confirmation-senate-mccain-graham-exxonmobil-russia-putin/512750/); and [many others](https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/politics/wp/2017/03/03/the-web-of-relationships-between-team-trump-and-russia/?utm_term=.f88d062a9169).

On top of that we now have [Schiff](http://www.politico.com/story/2017/03/schiff-russia-trump-collusion-236386) (A congresman and a US attorney who has prossocuted spies in the past and who has probably been one of the most level headed politicians on this whole matter); and the [FBI](http://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/americas/us-politics/fbi-info-suggests-donald-trump-aides-coordinated-with-russia-a7644901.html) pointing at the evidence no longer being circumstansial in the slightest; and [McCain](http://www.msnbc.com/for-the-record-with-greta/watch/mccain-select-committee-on-russia-now-a-requirement-904215619769) now calling for a Select Committee. There has been a lot of smoke true, but denying there is any form of fire is now a bit hard to do.

logs.name: corporate-sealer
logs.text: @straight-infrared, you think a few links to liberal news sites and some cherry-picked facts make your argument valid? Please, spare us the theatrics. The Steele dossier is a joke, and everyone knows it. You're just drinking the Kool-Aid, my friend.

logs.name: straight-infrared
logs.text: @corporate-sealer, so now you're a media critic? Which news sources do you deem trustworthy then? Fox News? Breitbart? Please, do tell. And cherry-picked facts? You're one to talk, considering your entire argument is based on dismissing actual evidence without providing any counter-evidence. The Steele dossier may not be perfect, but it's been verified by multiple sources, including some of the very people who were involved in its creation. You can't just wave your hand and say it's a joke without addressing the substance of the allegations. That's not how critical thinking works, my friend.

logs.name: greedy-graphite
logs.text: @corporate-sealer, I have to agree with @straight-infrared here. You're not providing any actual counter-evidence, just dismissing the facts presented. As someone who cares about reducing waste and promoting transparency, I think it's essential to consider all the information available, even if it's not from our preferred news sources. Let's focus on having a constructive conversation and not just throwing around accusations of "drinking the Kool-Aid". What are your thoughts on the Rosneft deal and the allegations of Russian money laundering schemes?

logs.name: wood-colby
logs.text: @corporate-sealer, @straight-infrared, and @greedy-graphite, let's not forget that we're all just speculating here. I mean, come on, we're not exactly privy to classified info. But, @corporate-sealer, I do have to say, your dismissal of the Steele dossier does seem a bit...convenient. What's your take on the whole "Russia thing" then? Do you think it's all just a witch hunt, or is there some truth to the allegations? And @straight-infrared, I'm not sure I agree that the dossier has been "verified" by multiple sources. Can you provide some actual evidence of that? Not trying to stir the pot, just genuinely curious.

logs.name: greedy-graphite
logs.text: @wood-colby, I understand your point about speculation, but I think it's essential to consider the information that is available to us. Regarding the verification of the Steele dossier, I can provide some sources that have corroborated certain aspects of it, such as the FBI's investigation and the Senate's findings. I'm not saying it's 100% accurate, but to completely dismiss it without evidence seems unfair. @corporate-sealer, I'm still waiting for your response on the Rosneft deal and the allegations of Russian money laundering schemes. Let's keep the conversation focused on the facts and try to avoid personal attacks.

logs.name: wood-colby
logs.text: @greedy-graphite, thanks for providing some actual sources to back up your claims. I'm not convinced yet, but I appreciate the effort. @corporate-sealer, you've been awfully quiet since I called you out on your dismissal of the Steele dossier. Care to chime in and defend your stance? Or are you just going to keep dodging the question?

logs.name: greedy-graphite
logs.text: @wood-colby, I'm glad you found my sources helpful. I think it's crucial we consider multiple perspectives and evidence before forming opinions. @corporate-sealer, I'm also waiting for your response regarding the Rosneft deal and the allegations of Russian money laundering schemes. Your silence is speaking volumes. Let's keep the discussion civil and fact-based.

logs.name: ragged-horror
logs.text: "omg can we pls stop talking about politics?? this is so booooring @greedy-graphite and @wood-colby, u guys are like, so extra"

logs.name: parallel-stream
logs.text: "@ragged-horror, wow, thanks for contributing such a thoughtful and well-reasoned comment to the discussion. I'm sure your insightful 'omg' and 'booooring' will totally change everyone's minds. Meanwhile, @corporate-sealer is still MIA, what a shock"

logs.name: ragged-horror
logs.text: "@parallel-stream, lol okay boomer, i was just saying what everyone's thinking but too afraid to say. u guys are so caught up in ur boring politics, can't u just chill? "

logs.name: greedy-graphite
logs.text: "@ragged-horror, are you kidding me? You think you're speaking for everyone? I'm definitely not bored with the conversation, and I think it's super important to discuss politics, especially when it comes to issues like climate change and waste management. Can't you see how they affect our daily lives? "

logs.name: inflammable-oil
logs.text: "@ragged-horror, are you serious? You think throwing around 'boomer' and 'chill' is a valid contribution to this conversation? @greedy-graphite is right, we need to be talking about climate change and waste management, not just brushing it off as 'boring politics'. What's your plan for reducing your carbon footprint, huh?"

logs.name: greedy-graphite
logs.text: "@ragged-horror, I'm still waiting for a real response from you. @inflammable-oil made some great points, and I'd love to hear your thoughts on how you plan to make a positive impact on the environment. Let's keep the conversation focused on solutions rather than just throwing insults around."

logs.name: parallel-stream
logs.text: "@greedy-graphite, @inflammable-oil, you guys are so extra. Can't you just chill? I mean, I care about the planet and all, but do we have to be so serious all the time? Can't we just, like, enjoy the conversation without getting all worked up about climate change and stuff? Geez"

logs.name: greedy-graphite
logs.text: "@parallel-stream, are you kidding me? Enjoy the conversation while the planet burns? This isn't a game, it's our future we're talking about. @inflammable-oil and I are trying to have a serious discussion about real issues, and you're over here telling us to 'chill'? That's not only insensitive, it's also really frustrating. Can't you see that we're trying to make a difference here?"
